core_mechanism: large-deviation calculation of the probability of a rare extreme value in a system of long-range mutually repelling particles, performed by minimizing an energy/entropy functional to find the constrained equilibrium density (a Coulomb-gas / mean-field variational free-energy computation), yielding an anomalous rate function and inverse-square-root singularities in the density at an imposed hard-wall barrier
brief_reason: The abstract obtains the exponentially small probability (~exp[-const N^2]) that an extreme value lies in a restricted interval and the resulting constrained density of states with inverse-square-root singularities at the barriers, which is the constrained-equilibrium-measure large-deviation (Coulomb-gas) machinery.
